concept_ref: §3 row 5 — 'One test per condition is enough for MCDC'

scenario.title: Simple Safety Gate
scenario.narrative: A safety system activates when sensor sensorTriggered is triggered AND buttonPressed is pressed.

claim: This 3-test set satisfies MCDC for both conditions.
charges: The defendant function activates a safety system only when sensorTriggered AND buttonPressed are both true.
The defendant submits a 3-test suite — (T,T), (F,T), (T,F) — and claims MC/DC coverage for the predicate `sensorTriggered && buttonPressed`.
The court must determine whether each condition has a valid independence pair within the submitted suite.
hints: An independence pair fixes every condition except one and the outcome must flip.
For sensorTriggered: which two tests differ ONLY in sensorTriggered and produce different outcomes? Same question for buttonPressed.

sensorTriggered's pair: T1 vs T2 (only sensorTriggered flips, outcome flips). buttonPressed's pair: T1 vs T3 (only buttonPressed flips, outcome flips). Three tests are enough here because T1 is shared.

seeded_faults.description: buttonPressed check is bypassed when sensorTriggered is triggered

misconceptions.explanation_md: You tested each condition in isolation. MCDC requires *paired* test cases where one condition changes while all others remain fixed and the decision outcome flips.
